_comment: Example hook registration for claude-kit. Paths resolve via $CLAUDE_CONFIG_DIR (default ~/.claude), where install.sh symlinks the hooks. install.sh copies this to your settings.json ONLY if you don't already have one. Two kinds of hook live here and they are NOT all advisory: GUARD hooks (prevent-laziness, workaround-spiral-detector, commit-message-audit) emit a block decision when they catch a violation — review each before relying on it; REMINDER hooks (error-diagnosis-trigger, session-retro-reminder) only inject advice. Each hook is registered ONLY on the tool events its script actually handles (see the matcher on each block) — a mismatch silently disables it.

hooks.description: Enforces CLAUDE.md § Session Hygiene (rule h): block a commit message referencing entities/paths absent from the staged diff

Enforces CLAUDE.md § Build Discipline + Effort Standards (rules a/b/g): Bash bypasses of a Makefile target + dangerous ops; Write/Edit stubs/TODOs/disabled tests. Ladder R3/R4 bash-retry warn@4/block@5
Enforces CLAUDE.md § Escalation ladder R3/R4 (warn@3/block@4): repeated edits to the same build-config file (workaround churn)
Enforces CLAUDE.md § Diagnosis Protocol (rule d, ladder R1): after a command failure, diagnose root cause before retrying
Enforces CLAUDE.md § Session Hygiene: suggest running /retro after git commit operations
